sanskrit:
यद्यप्येते न पश्यन्ति लोभोपहतचेतसः।
कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं मित्रद्रोहे च पातकम्॥
transliteration:
yady apy ete na paśyanti lobhopahata-cetasaḥ |
kula-kṣaya-kṛtaṁ doṣaṁ mitra-drohe ca pātakam ||
literal_meaning: Even though / these (people) / do not / see / greed-afflicted / minds / family-destruction / produced / fault / in betraying friends / and / sin
contextual_meaning: Arjuna observes that greed has clouded the enemy’s moral vision so they cannot see the sin in destroying their own kin or betraying allies; he questions why he, who sees the harm clearly, should participate.
psychological_state: Moral projection—Arjuna distances himself from the ‘greedy other’ to stabilize his own wavering identity; a moment of splitting before integration.
trigger_scenario: A team member refuses to join a lucrative project that would bankrupt competitors who are also old friends, asking ‘If they’re blind to the damage, why should I be?’
anchor_text: Greed blinds the heart to the sin of destroying family.
